description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, tilted photovoltaic panel array mounted on a robust, rectangular base structure. The solar panels are arranged in a single, continuous row and are supported by a visible frame, likely made of metal, which elevates and angles the panels for optimal sunlight exposure. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and possibly reinforced concrete, providing stability and durability. The surface of the panels is dark and reflective, characteristic of crystalline silicon or similar photovoltaic materials, while the base and surrounding frame are predominantly black and gray. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling on the exterior, and the structure is not designed for entry or traversal by people. The installation is suitable for placement in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A large, tilted solar panel array mounted on a sturdy rectangular base, with a dark reflective surface and robust frame, designed for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the tilted solar panel and the depth of the supporting base.
description_view_1: Wide, frontal view highlighting the full expanse of the solar panel array and the solid, block-like base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the angled support structure and the sloped orientation of the solar panel, with base details visible.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the length of the base and the steep tilt of the solar panel above it.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a robust rectangular base and metal frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6-8 panels
